source.title: The Role of Hippocampal Replay in Memory and Planning

confidence_rationale: The review explicitly proposes that replay function changes dynamically with task demands and arousal level. This is the closest published claim to MECH-269's context-dependent anchor/probe mixture, short of a paper testing verisimilitude-gated selection directly. Mapping is still not mechanism-for-mechanism, but it is tighter than Foster 2017 because this review actively argues for task-dependent functional switching rather than just cataloguing variety.
failure_signatures: If replay function were task-invariant (same role in consolidation, recall, and planning regardless of context), MECH-269's context-modulated anchor/probe ratio would lose its biological motivation.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal replay supports multiple cognitive functions (memory consolidation, recall, working memory, planning, reinforcement learning) and its function changes dynamically according to task demands and arousal level.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-269 reads this as direct support for context-modulated anchor/probe mixture: if replay's function genuinely shifts with task demand, then the gating mechanism selecting which proposer mode is active under which condition must exist, and regional verisimilitude is a plausible candidate gate.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review identifies task-modulated replay function but does not specify the gating variable. Their 'task demand and arousal' could be encoded by many things (global precision, salience, dopamine tone) other than per-stream verisimilitude. Treating this as MECH-269 support requires granting that the unresolved gating variable could plausibly be regional V_s.
mapping.source_context: Rodent hippocampal electrophysiology; primarily CA1 place-cell replay studies across spatial tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Current Biology review with senior authors (Barry lab, UCL). Mapping is closer than pure phenomenology reviews because this paper actively proposes context-switching of replay function, which is the same architectural shape as MECH-269.
